channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé sends Les Bleus into quarterfinals after hard-fought win over Paraguay
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé sends Les Bleus into quarterfinals after hard-fought win over Paraguay
item.description: France edged past a stubborn and physical Paraguay side thanks to a Kylian Mbappé penalty (1-0), sealing their place in the World Cup 2026 quarterfinals after a frustrating but dominant display. They will take on Morocco on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Hezbollah, Hamas and Houthi officials attend funeral ceremonies for Iran's Khamenei
item.description: Tens of thousands of mourners flocked to Tehran's Grand Mosalla Mosque to pay their respects to late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday. Among them were representatives from Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis of Yemen, members of the "axis of resistance" that Iran has long supported as part of its years-long struggle against Israel.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Paraguay-France: Mbappé penalty puts Les Bleus ahead in tense Round of 16 clash
item.description: France edged past Paraguay 1-0 in the Round of 16 of the World Cup 2026, maintaining their perfect tournament record and moving one step closer to another global title. Les Bleus’ dominant group stage performances have established them as genuine contenders, while Paraguay’s resistance was eventually broken by a Kylian Mbappé penalty after a tense and physical contest. Relive our liveblog for minute-to-minute commentary.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ounahi fires Morocco to quarter-finals with decisive brace
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ounahi fires Morocco to quarter-finals with decisive brace
item.description: Morocco secured their passage to the quarter-finals with a 3-0 victory over Canada in Houston, with Selim Ounahi's double proving decisive. The Lions of the Atlas overcame a difficult first half to eventually overwhelm their North American hosts and progress as one of the tournament's standout sides.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Armenia’s defiant drag scene braves pushback
item.description:
Armenia sits at the crossroads of Europe, Asia and the Middle East. While in much of the European Union, the drag scene is mainstream, in Armenia it remains underground. Performers face threats of violence, stigma, police pressure and even rejection by their own families.
Despite this, drag queens have built a vibrant, bustling community that has attracted both local Armenians and international crowds. They make up a passionate and determined microculture challenging ultraconservative norms.
And this avant garde scene is, in turn, being influenced by Armenia’s evolving political landscape.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Vingegaard takes early Tour de France lead as Visma win opening stage in Barcelona
item.description: Danish cyclist Jonas Vingegaard's Visma-Lease a Bike outfit came first in the opening team time-trial of this year's Tour de France in Barcelona on Saturday. Slovenian rival Tadej Pogacar, who put in a strong performance, is hoping to become the fifth cyclist to win the Tour de France five times.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Curtis Young on America's "diffcult moment in the mirror"
item.description: This weekend, the United States marks 250 years since the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, one of the most influential political texts in modern history. Its assertion that governments derive their power from the consent of the governed, and that all people are endowed with the rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness, has inspired democratic movements around the world.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Tens of thousands rally in France for a comprehensive law to fight sexual violence
item.description: The shockwave following the rape and murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna in May has continued to echo across France, with tens of thousands of people across the country marching on Saturday in support of what organisers call a comprehensive law against sexual violence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Rebel attacks in northern Mali: Some targets meant as 'diversions', expert says
item.description: The Malian army said Saturday that several northern towns, including Gao and Sévaré, were targeted by rebels. The statement came as a rebel group announced a new offensive to capture a town in the country's north. Andrew Lebovich, a research fellow at Clingendael's Conflict Research Unit, said some of the targets were intended more as "diversions" than as primary objectives, unlike strategically important towns such as Anefis.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France's first detected Ebola patient recovers, leaves hospital
item.description: A doctor who was being treated for Ebola in France after having tested positive following a trip to the Democratic Republic of Congo has recovered and is out of hospital, Health Minister Stéphanie Rist said in a statement Saturday. It was the first time that French authorities had detected the deadly fever in France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: A divided US celebrates 250th birthday as heatwave bears down on East Coast
item.description: The US is celebrating the 250th anniversary of the country's independence on Saturday under sweltering temperatures, with a massive fireworks show planned in Washington following a speech by US President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France to face Paraguay as World Cup last 16 gets under way
item.description: France are bracing for furnace-like temperatures when they face Paraguay in Philadelphia in the World Cup's last 16 on Saturday as co-hosts Canada prepare for a daunting date with African giants Morocco in Houston. With a brutal heatwave roasting swathes of the United States as it celebrates July 4th, Les Bleus take on Paraguay at Lincoln Financial Field against a backdrop of extreme heat warnings.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: New wave of coordinated rebel attacks hits Mali
item.description: Jihadists and their separatist Tuareg allies hit Mali with fresh coordinated attacks Saturday, striking multiple towns and a prison just months after hobbling the country's military junta with a similar wave of assaults. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle explains.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mali government says rebels attacking northern towns
item.description: The Malian army said Saturday that several northern towns, including Gao and Sévaré, were targeted by rebels. The statement came as a rebel group announced a new offensive to capture a northern town. Mohamed Elmaouloud Ramadane, a spokesperson for ​the Azawad Liberation Front (FLA), said in a Facebook post that the town of Anefis was being targeted by the separatists. FRANCE 24's Wassim Nasr reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran holds funeral for Ali Khamenei: Attendees 'hardline factions' of society, specialist says
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran on Saturday for the start of a dayslong funeral for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Tara Kangarlou, a global affairs journalist and author of The Heartbeat of Iran, said most attendees were "more emboldened, hardline factions of the society," making the crowd unrepresentative of broader public attitudes toward the regime.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pop star Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married Friday night at Madison Square Garden, where actor Adam Sandler was the surprising officiant at a ceremony and Stevie Nicks performed among a crowd packed with stars of sports and entertainment.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Tour de France kicks off in Barcelona
item.description: The annual men's cycling race, Tour de France, kicked off on Saturday in Barcelona amid a festive atmosphere.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026 : Cape Verde miss out on greatest upset
item.description: Newcomers Cape Verde almost created the upset of the century by forcing the reigning champions Argentina into extra time, unfortunately the blue sharks conceded in extra time. Argentina will therefore face Egypt in the round of 16. There was not much in it between Australia's Socceroos' and the Egyptian Pharoes, the two teams went all the way to penalties, where Egypt proved extremely efficient, scoring every single shot from the penalty spot. An early Colombian goal meanwhile proved insurmountable for Ghana, the black stars will head home without any shot on target in that knockout game. France gears up for their game against Panama, and Canada face Morocco in the first round of 16 fixture.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: New Zealand's Rennie expresses 'relief' as All Blacks edge narrow win over France
item.description: The All Blacks began their 2026 Nations Championship tenure with a narrow win over France on Saturday, with freshly appointed coach Dave Rennie promising the team was only going to get better as they head into future matches.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Zelensky denies Russian capture of key eastern city
item.description: Ukraine still controls the strategically important eastern city of Kostiantynivka, President ​Volodymyr Zelensky and the General Staff said on Saturday, rejecting Russian claims that it has been captured. Russia's military told President ​Vladimir Putin ‌on Friday that its forces had taken ⁠control of Kostiantynivka, a target that Moscow has long sought in its advance ‌through the Donetsk region.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump speech at Mount Rushmore: Making it clear 'who's in and who's out', expert says
item.description: President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of American independence on Friday with soaring rhetoric about American exceptionalism before veering into a darkly political speech with warnings about a sinister threat of communism that evoked one of the country’s ugliest chapters. Ian Reifowitz, SUNY Distinguished Professor of history at SUNY-Empire State University, said Trump has "one goal - make himself more popular and more powerful," as he pointed to how Trump has politicised the celebrations through his speech.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
